title: Timer wraparound bug in parent expiration causes incorrect expiry
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/dodag.c:398

The age calculation `uint32_t age = now - p->last_updated;` does not handle timestamp wraparound correctly. For a 32-bit millisecond counter, this wraps after ~49.7 days.

When `now` wraps past `last_updated` (e.g., now=1000, last_updated=UINT32_MAX-1000), the subtraction produces a very large positive value due to unsigned arithmetic, causing entries to be incorrectly expired even if they were recently updated.

Impact: After ~50 days of runtime, parent expiration logic may incorrectly expire valid parents or fail to expire stale ones.

Fix: Use saturating/wraparound-aware age calculation, similar to how `lichen_trickle_interval_end()` handles overflow in trickle.c.
notes:
Commit: 9090d61e9
Deadline-based signed diff `(int32_t)(now - deadline) >= 0` already implemented in lichen_rpl_dodag_expire_parents() (lines 413-414). Matches trickle.c pattern and C/Rust/Python consistency. Prevents ~49-day expiry bug. Verified no other unsigned subtractions. P2 fixed.
close_reason: Wraparound-safe signed timestamp comparison implemented; commit 9090d61e9
